articles.title: Spike in brain attacking autoantibodies linked to early COVID-19 pandemic
articles.titleKo: COVID-19 팬데믹 초기와 연관된 뇌 공격 자가항체의 급증
articles.summary: A massive review of laboratory data reveals that severe autoantibody attacks on the brain surged during the initial COVID-19 outbreak before falling back to baseline as population immunity and vaccination rates increased.
articles.aiSummary:
This study examined a surge in brain-attacking autoantibodies (antibodies where the immune system attacks the brain) observed during the early COVID-19 pandemic. Researchers analyzed large-scale laboratory data collected from the pandemic's onset through subsequent periods to track changes in autoantibody levels over time. Results showed that autoantibodies targeting the brain increased significantly during the initial COVID-19 outbreak but returned to baseline levels as vaccination rates rose and population immunity increased. This finding suggests a potential link between COVID-19 infection and the development of neurological autoimmune conditions. However, the study was based on retrospective data analysis and could not establish direct causation between elevated autoantibodies and actual neurological symptoms.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:

articles.title: Neuronal electricality founded in murburn-thermodynamic principles: 1. Background and basic theoretical formulation
articles.titleKo: 신경세포 전기성의 뮤번 열역학적 원리 기반: 1. 배경 및 기본 이론 공식화
articles.summary: arXiv:2604.24772v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Trans-membrane gradients and fluxes of cations (H+, Na+, K+, etc.) were deemed to be the rationale of electrical activities of aerobic cells/organelles, as per classical perceptions. Murburn concept (an umbrella of theorization based in stochastic redox processes) has afforded novel models for various metabolic, bioenergetic and electrophysiological outcomes. Herein, the foundational mechanistic formalisms for the electrical activities of neurons that lead signal relay along the axonal length are provided. Electron Holding potential (EHP), a dime
articles.aiSummary:
This study revisits classical theories on how electrical activities are generated in neurons and proposes a novel explanatory model. Traditionally, the concentration gradient and movement of cations (H⁺, Na⁺, K⁺, etc.) across cell membranes have explained neuronal electrical signal generation. The research team applies the "murburn" concept—a theoretical framework based on stochastic redox processes—to reinterpret neuronal electrical activity. They introduce a new concept called Electron Holding Potential (EHP) to explain the mechanism of signal transmission along axons. This research represents an attempt to re-establish foundational theory in neurobiology, offering the potential to provide an integrated explanation of metabolic, bioenergetic, and electrophysiological phenomena. However, as this remains a theoretical proposal stage, experimental validation is required and additional research is necessary to gain consensus from the existing neurobiology community.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
본 연구는 신경 세포의 전기적 활동이 어떻게 발생하는지에 대한 기존 이론을 재검토하고 새로운 설명 모델을 제시합니다. 종래에는 세포막을 통한 양이온(H⁺, Na⁺, K⁺ 등)의 농도 차이와 이동이 신경세포의 전기적 신호 생성을 설명했습니다. 연구팀은 "머번(murburn)" 개념이라는 무작위 산화환원 과정에 기반한 이론적 틀을 적용하여 신경의 전기적 활동을 새롭게 해석했습니다. 특히 전자 보유 전위(EHP, Electron Holding Potential)라는 새로운 개념을 도입하여 축삭을 따라 신호가 전달되는 메커니즘을 제시합니다. 이 연구는 신경 생물학의 기초 이론을 재정립하려는 시도로, 대사, 에너지 생성, 전기생리학 현상을 통합적으로 설명할 수 있는 가능성을 제공합니다. 다만 이는 이론적 제안 단계로, 실험적 검증이 필요하며 기존 신경생물학 커뮤니티의 합의를 얻기 위한 추가 연구가 필수적입니다.

articles.title: One-shot emergency psychiatric triage across 15 frontier AI chatbots
articles.titleKo: 15개 최전선 AI 챗봇을 통한 일회성 응급 정신과 분류
articles.summary: arXiv:2604.25415v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: AI chatbots are increasingly used for health advice, but their performance in psychiatric triage remains undercharacterized. Psychiatric triage is particularly challenging because urgency must often be inferred from thoughts, behavior, and context rather than from objective findings. We evaluated the performance of 15 frontier AI chatbots on psychiatric triage from realistic single-message disclosures using 112 clinical vignettes, each paired with 1 of 4 original benchmark triage labels: A, routine; B, assessment within 1 week; C, assessment with
articles.aiSummary:
This study evaluated how well 15 frontier AI chatbots perform in psychiatric emergency triage, a task increasingly required as these tools are used for health advice. Psychiatric triage is particularly challenging because clinicians must infer urgency from patients' thoughts, behaviors, and contextual information rather than objective clinical findings. Researchers assessed performance using 112 clinical vignettes presented as single-message disclosures, each paired with one of four benchmark triage classifications: A (routine care), B (assessment within 1 week), C (urgent assessment needed), and D (immediate response required). This research provides crucial insights into whether healthcare providers and patients can reliably trust AI chatbots in mental health crises. However, the study's limitation of using single-message scenarios may not fully capture the complexity of real clinical situations, and continuous updates to chatbot models mean performance may differ from the time of evaluation.

##

본 연구는 의료 상담에 점점 더 많이 사용되는 AI 챗봇들이 정신과 응급 상황 분류에서 어떤 수준의 성능을 보이는지 평가했습니다. 정신과 응급 분류는 객관적 검사 결과보다 환자의 생각, 행동, 상황 맥락에서 긴급성을 판단해야 하기 때문에 특히 어렵습니다. 연구팀은 15개의 최신 AI 챗봇을 대상으로 112개의 임상 사례 시나리오를 사용하여 성능을 평가했으며, 각 시나리오는 일상적 상담(A), 1주일 내 평가(B), 긴급 평가 필요(C), 즉시 대응(D) 등 4가지 기준 분류 중 하나로 레이블링되었습니다. 이 연구는 의료 제공자와 환자들이 정신과 위기 상황에서 AI 챗봇을 얼마나 신뢰할 수 있는지 파악하는 데 중요한 실마리를 제공합니다. 다만, 단일 메시지 기반의 평가로 실제 임상 상황의 복잡성을 완전히 반영하지 못했을 수 있으며, 챗봇의 지속적인 업데이트로 인해 연구 시점의 성능이 현재와 다를 수 있다는 한계가 있습니다.

articles.title: A geometry aware framework enhances noninvasive mapping of whole human brain dynamics
articles.titleKo: 기하학적 특성을 고려한 프레임워크를 통한 인간 뇌 전체 역학의 비침습적 매핑 향상
articles.summary: arXiv:2604.25592v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Non-invasive electrophysiology lacks methods that accurately reconstruct whole-brain spatiotemporal dynamics while incorporating individual cortical geometry, leaving current electroencephalography and magnetoencephalography source imaging limited by simplistic or biologically implausible priors. Here, we show that embedding participant-specific Geometric Basis Functions (GBFs), eigenmodes derived from each individual's cortical surface, provides a powerful anatomic constraint that resolves the inverse problem and improves reconstruction fidelity
articles.aiSummary:
Non-invasive brain imaging techniques like electroencephalography (EEG) and magnetoencephalography (MEG) struggle to accurately reconstruct whole-brain activity while accounting for individual differences in cortical geometry. Existing methods have used oversimplified or biologically implausible assumptions that limit reconstruction accuracy. This study proposes a novel geometric-aware framework that incorporates Geometric Basis Functions (GBFs)—eigenmodes derived from each participant's individual cortical surface geometry. The framework significantly improves the reconstruction fidelity of whole-brain spatiotemporal dynamics from non-invasive electrophysiological signals. By leveraging individual-specific brain geometry as an anatomical constraint, the method resolves the inverse problem more effectively than conventional approaches. This advancement could enhance the reliability of non-invasive brain imaging for clinical diagnosis and neuroscientific research. However, validation with larger clinical datasets and improvements in computational efficiency remain necessary.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
뇌 활동을 비침습적으로 측정하는 뇌파(EEG)와 자기뇌파(MEG) 기술은 측정 신호로부터 뇌 전체의 활동을 재구성하는 데 어려움을 겪고 있습니다. 기존 방법들은 개인의 뇌 표면 기하학적 구조를 충분히 반영하지 못했습니다. 이 연구는 각 개인의 뇌 피질 표면에서 추출한 고유 기하학적 함수(Geometric Basis Functions, GBFs)를 사용하는 새로운 프레임워크를 제안했습니다. 연구팀은 이 방법이 뇌파 신호로부터 뇌 전체의 시공간 역학(spatiotemporal dynamics)을 더 정확하게 재구성할 수 있음을 보여주었습니다. 개인별 뇌 기하학 구조를 활용함으로써 역문제 해결의 정확성을 크게 향상시켰습니다. 이 기술은 임상 진단 및 뇌 연구에서 비침습적 뇌 영상의 신뢰성을 높일 수 있는 잠재력을 가지고 있습니다. 다만, 추가적인 임상 데이터를 통한 검증과 계산 효율성 개선이 필요합니다.

##

##
keyPoints: **혁신적 방법론**: 개인별 뇌 피질의 기하학적 구조를 반영한 고유 기하학적 함수(GBF)를 사용하여 기존의 단순화된 가정을 극복 / Revolutionary methodology using individual-specific Geometric Basis Functions derived from cortical surface geometry to overcome oversimplified assumptions in conventional methods

**역문제 해결 개선**: 복잡한 뇌파 신호로부터 뇌 활동을 역으로 추론하는 문제(inverse problem)의 정확성을 크게 향상시킴 / Significantly improves the solution of the inverse problem in reconstructing brain activity from complex electrophysiological signals

articles.title: Independent-Component-Based Encoding Models of Brain Activity During Story Comprehension
articles.titleKo: 이야기 이해 중 뇌 활동의 독립성분 기반 인코딩 모델
articles.summary: arXiv:2604.24942v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: Encoding models provide a powerful framework for linking continuous stimulus features to neural activity; however, traditional voxelwise approaches are limited by measurement noise, inter-subject variability, and redundancy arising from spatially correlated voxels encoding overlapping neural signals. Here, we propose an independent component (IC)-based encoding framework that dissociates stimulus-driven and noise-driven signals in fMRI data. We decompose continuous fMRI data from naturalistic story listening into ICs using one subset of the dat
articles.aiSummary:
This study proposes a novel neuroimaging analysis method to more accurately link brain activity with external stimuli during story listening. Traditional fMRI analysis approaches suffer from measurement noise, inter-subject variability, and difficulty in distinguishing overlapping signals from different brain regions. The researchers developed a new encoding model using Independent Component Analysis (ICA) to separate stimulus-driven signals from noise in brain activity data. The experiment involved measuring brain activity via fMRI while participants listened to narratives, training the model on a subset of the data, and validating performance on the remaining data. This innovative approach is expected to explain brain activity more accurately than conventional voxel-based methods. The significance lies in better understanding language processing in natural brain activity conditions, with potential applications in neurolinguistics and cognitive neuroscience research.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
이 연구는 뇌 활동과 외부 자극(스토리 청취) 간의 관계를 더 정확히 파악하기 위한 새로운 뇌영상 분석 방법을 제시합니다. 기존의 뇌MRI 분석 방식은 측정 오류, 개인차, 그리고 뇌의 여러 영역이 겹치는 신호를 제대로 구분하지 못하는 한계가 있었습니다. 연구진은 독립 성분 분석(ICA)이라는 기술을 사용하여 뇌신호를 자극에 반응하는 신호와 잡음으로 분리하는 새로운 인코딩 모델을 개발했습니다. 실험은 참가자들이 이야기를 듣는 동안의 뇌 활동을 fMRI로 측정하고, 데이터의 일부로 모델을 학습한 후 나머지 데이터로 성능을 검증하는 방식으로 진행되었습니다. 이 새로운 방법은 기존 복셀 기반 접근법보다 뇌 활동을 더 정확하게 설명할 수 있는 것으로 기대됩니다. 연구의 의의는 자연스러운 뇌 활동 상황에서 언어 처리 과정을 더 잘 이해하는 데 있으며, 향후 신경언어학이나 인지신경과학 연구에 활용될 수 있습니다.

keyPoints: **독립 성분 분석 활용** / Independent Component Analysis (ICA) utilization for dissociating stimulus-driven and noise-driven signals in fMRI data

articles.title: Shared music listening synchronizes brain activity
articles.titleKo: 음악을 함께 듣는 것이 뇌 활동을 동기화시킨다
articles.summary: Listening to music with a friend syncs up your emotional and brain responses. According to a recent Cortex study, sharing a musical experience increases the moment-to-moment similarity of subjective pleasure and enhances neural alignment between listeners.
articles.aiSummary:
A recent study published in Cortex reveals that listening to music with a friend synchronizes emotional and brain responses between listeners. The research demonstrates that shared musical experiences increase moment-to-moment similarity in subjective pleasure and enhance neural alignment between participants. This study goes beyond viewing music appreciation as an individual activity, showing that listening together creates emotional and neurological synchronization between people. The findings suggest that music has neurobiological mechanisms that can strengthen social bonding and potentially enhance empathy among listeners. The synchronized brain activity indicates that people literally align their neural responses when sharing a musical experience. However, the article lacks specific details about participant numbers, experimental design specifics, and quantitative measures of neural synchronization, necessitating further investigation of the original research.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
친구와 함께 음악을 듣는 경험이 뇌 활동을 동기화시킨다는 연구 결과가 발표되었습니다. 최근 Cortex 저널에 게재된 이 연구는 음악 감상이 단순한 개인적 경험을 넘어 함께 듣는 사람들 간의 감정적·신경적 동기화를 일으킨다는 것을 보여줍니다. 연구팀은 함께 음악을 청취하는 조건에서 참가자들의 쾌감(주관적 즐거움)이 순간순간 더 높은 유사성을 나타낼 수 있음을 발견했습니다. 특히 두 청취자 간의 신경 활동 정렬(neural alignment)이 향상되어 뇌 수준에서 실제로 동기화가 일어남을 확인했습니다. 이는 음악이 사람들 간의 사회적 결합을 강화하고 공감 능력을 증진시킬 수 있는 신경생물학적 기제를 제시합니다. 다만 해당 기사에서는 구체적인 참가자 수, 실험 설계 세부사항, 신경 동기화의 정량적 수치가 명시되지 않아 추가적인 정보 확인이 필요합니다.

##

The findings suggest that music has neurobiological mechanisms that can strengthen social bonding and potentially enhance empathy among listeners. The synchronized brain activity indicates that people literally align their neural responses when sharing a musical experience. However, the article lacks specific details about participant numbers, experimental design specifics, and quantitative measures of neural synchronization, necessitating further investigation of the original research.

keyPoints: **음악 청취의 사회적 동기화**: 친구와 함께 음악을 들을 때 참가자들의 순간적 쾌감이 더욱 유사해지며, 개인적 감상과 다른 신경생물학적 반응이 일어남 / **Social Synchronization Through Music**: Listening to music with a friend increases moment-to-moment similarity in subjective pleasure and produces distinct neurobiological responses compared to individual listening

articles.title: MIT scientists turn chaotic laser light into powerful brain imaging tool
articles.titleKo: MIT 과학자들, 혼란스러운 레이저 빛을 강력한 뇌 영상 도구로 전환
articles.summary: Scientists at MIT discovered that chaotic laser light can spontaneously form a highly focused beam instead of scattering—if the conditions are just right. This “pencil beam” enabled them to image the blood-brain barrier in 3D at speeds 25 times faster than existing techniques. The method also lets researchers watch how drugs move into brain cells in real time. It could dramatically accelerate the development of treatments for neurological diseases.
articles.aiSummary:
MIT scientists discovered that chaotic laser light can spontaneously form a highly focused beam under specific conditions instead of scattering randomly. Using this "pencil beam," researchers achieved 25 times faster imaging speeds of the blood-brain barrier in 3D compared to existing techniques. The novel method enables real-time observation of how drugs move into brain cells, potentially accelerating the development of treatments for neurological diseases. This breakthrough represents an innovative fusion of laser physics and neuroscience that could open new avenues for brain disease research. The technology promises to be a valuable tool for studying neurological conditions such as Alzheimer's and Parkinson's disease. However, additional validation is required before clinical application, and demonstrating practical utility in biological systems remains a key future challenge.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:

articles.title: Your dreams aren’t random. Here’s what’s really happening
articles.titleKo: 당신의 꿈은 무작위가 아니다. 실제로 일어나고 있는 일은 이것이다
articles.summary: Dreams are more structured than they seem, shaped by both personal traits and real-world experiences. Researchers found that the brain doesn’t just replay daily life—it reshapes it into imaginative, sometimes surreal scenarios. People who mind-wander more tend to have fragmented dreams, while those who value dreams experience richer ones. Even major events like the pandemic changed dream content, making it more emotional and restrictive.
articles.aiSummary: Dreams are not random but are structured by personal psychological traits and real-world experiences, according to new research findings. Researchers discovered that the brain does not simply replay daily life but reshapes it into imaginative, surreal scenarios. The study revealed that individuals who frequently mind-wander tend to have fragmented dreams, while those who value their dreams experience richer and more complex dream content. Notably, major societal events like the pandemic altered dream content, making dreams more emotionally intense and restrictive in nature. This research is significant as it demonstrates that dreams serve as a window reflecting our psychological state and the stress of our actual lives. However, the study has limitations, including difficulties in quantitatively measuring individual dream experiences and reliance on self-reported data from participants.
articles.aiSummaryKo: 꿈은 무작위로 발생하는 것이 아니라 개인의 심리적 특성과 실제 경험에 의해 구조적으로 형성된다는 연구 결과가 발표되었다. 연구진은 뇌가 일상을 단순히 재생하는 것이 아니라 상상력을 더해 초현실적인 시나리오로 재구성한다는 것을 발견했다. 연구에 따르면 일상에서 마음이 자주 흐트러지는 사람들은 단편적인 꿈을 꾸는 경향이 있으며, 반대로 꿈을 소중히 여기는 사람들은 더 풍부하고 복잡한 꿈을 경험한다. 더 흥미로운 점은 팬데믹과 같은 사회적 대사건이 꿈의 내용을 변화시켜 더욱 감정적이고 제한적으로 만들었다는 것이다. 이 연구는 꿈이 우리의 심리 상태와 실제 삶의 스트레스를 반영하는 창(窓)임을 보여준다는 점에서 의의가 있다. 다만 개별 꿈 경험을 정량적으로 측정하기 어렵고, 참가자의 자기보고에 의존하는 한계가 있다.

articles.title: This hidden kind of stress may be damaging your memory as you age
articles.titleKo: 나이가 들면서 당신의 기억력을 손상시킬 수 있는 숨겨진 스트레스
articles.summary: A new study reveals that internalizing stress—especially feelings of hopelessness—may significantly speed up memory decline in older Chinese Americans. Surprisingly, factors like community support didn’t show the same impact. Researchers say cultural pressures and stereotypes may cause emotional struggles to go unnoticed and untreated. The findings suggest that targeted, culturally sensitive stress relief could play a powerful role in preserving cognitive health.
articles.aiSummary:
This study was conducted to identify factors contributing to memory decline with age, focusing on older Chinese Americans. Researchers investigated the relationship between internalized stress—particularly feelings of hopelessness—and memory deterioration in this population. Key findings revealed that internalized emotions like hopelessness significantly accelerated memory decline, while surprisingly, external factors such as community support showed less impact. Researchers explain that cultural pressures and stereotypes render these emotional struggles invisible, leading to missed treatment opportunities. The findings suggest that culturally tailored stress relief interventions could play a crucial role in preserving cognitive health among aging populations. However, the study's focus on a specific ethnic group limits generalization to other populations, which represents a notable limitation of the research.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
이 연구는 나이가 들면서 기억력이 저하되는 원인을 규명하기 위해 진행되었습니다. 연구팀은 중국계 미국인 고령자들을 대상으로 내재화된 스트레스(특히 절망감)와 기억력 감퇴 간의 관계를 조사했습니다. 연구 결과, 절망감 같은 내면화된 감정이 기억력 쇠퇴를 상당히 가속화하는 것으로 나타났으며, 놀랍게도 지역사회 지원과 같은 외부 요인은 유사한 영향을 미치지 않았습니다. 연구자들은 문화적 압박과 고정관념이 이러한 정서적 어려움을 눈에 띄지 않게 만들어 치료 기회를 놓치게 한다고 설명합니다. 이 발견은 문화에 맞춘 맞춤형 스트레스 완화가 인지 건강 보존에 중요한 역할을 할 수 있음을 시사합니다. 다만 이 연구는 특정 민족 집단을 중심으로 수행되어 다른 인구 집단에 대한 일반화에는 제한이 있을 수 있습니다.

articles.title: Brain halves become less alike as kids grow, especially in highly intelligent teens
articles.titleKo: 아이들이 자라면서 뇌의 양쪽이 달라진다, 특히 지능이 높은 청소년들에서
articles.summary: As children grow, the left and right hemispheres of their brains begin working more independently. A new study reveals that this division of mental labor happens at an accelerated rate in adolescents with superior intelligence scores.
articles.aiSummary:
This study examined how the left and right hemispheres of the brain become more specialized as children grow. Researchers analyzed brain imaging data from children and adolescents to measure functional differences between brain hemispheres across different ages. The key finding is that adolescents with superior intelligence scores show accelerated independent functioning of their left and right hemispheres compared to their peers. This means that the phenomenon of brain lateralization—where the two hemispheres take on increasingly distinct roles—occurs at a faster rate in highly intelligent teens. This research contributes to understanding the relationship between brain development and intelligence, while revealing important characteristics of cognitive development during adolescence. However, the study may be based on cross-sectional data, necessitating longitudinal follow-up research.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
이 연구는 뇌의 좌반구와 우반구가 성장 과정에서 어떻게 변화하는지 조사한 것입니다. 연구팀은 아동과 청소년 참가자들의 뇌 이미징 데이터를 분석하여 연령에 따른 뇌 반구 간의 기능적 차이를 측정했습니다. 핵심 발견은 일반 아동에 비해 지능이 높은 청소년들에서 좌반구와 우반구가 더 독립적으로 작동하는 경향이 두드러진다는 것입니다. 즉, 뇌의 두 반구가 서로 다른 역할을 더욱 분명히 하는 '뇌 반구화(lateralization)' 현상이 고지능 청소년에게서 가속화된다는 의미입니다. 이 연구는 뇌 발달과 지능 간의 관계를 이해하는 데 기여하며, 청소년의 인지 발달 특성을 규명합니다. 다만 연구는 횡단적 데이터에 기반했을 가능성이 있어 장기 추적 연구가 필요합니다.

articles.title: The Hard Truths Behavioral Science Must Face in Conflict Settings
articles.titleKo: 갈등 상황에서 행동과학이 직면해야 할 어려운 현실
articles.summary: Approximately 2 billion people live in conflict-affected areas. Yet the field of behavioral science has developed most of its theories, run most of its experiments, and built most of its interventions in stable contexts. When behavioral scientists do work in conflict settings, we tend to follow a familiar pattern: take what worked elsewhere, “localize” it, and evaluate it. But this isn’t enough. This is an urgent problem. Conflict is reshaping lives globally at an unprecedented scale. In 2024, 123 million people were forcibly displaced —the highest number on record. Civilian casualties in Gaza
articles.aiSummary:
Approximately 2 billion people live in conflict-affected areas, yet behavioral science has developed most of its theories, experiments, and interventions in stable contexts. When behavioral scientists conduct research in conflict settings, they typically apply methods that worked elsewhere through "localization," but this approach is insufficient. The article emphasizes that conflict is reshaping lives globally at an unprecedented scale, with 123 million people forcibly displaced in 2024—the highest number on record—and rising civilian casualties particularly in Gaza. Rather than simply adapting existing interventions, behavioral science must develop new theories and methodologies specifically designed for conflict environments. The research significance lies in highlighting the urgent need for context-specific solutions that account for the unique psychosocial factors in conflict zones. Current limitations stem from the field's heavy reliance on research conducted in stable environments, making it difficult to address the complex realities of conflict settings effectively.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
현재 약 20억 명의 사람들이 분쟁 지역에서 생활하고 있지만, 행동과학 분야의 대부분의 이론과 실험, 개입 방법은 안정적인 환경에서 개발되었다. 행동과학자들이 분쟁 지역에서 연구할 때도 다른 지역에서 효과가 있던 방법을 "현지화"하여 평가하는 익숙한 패턴을 따르는 경향이 있다. 그러나 이러한 접근 방식만으로는 충분하지 않다는 것이 이 연구의 핵심 주장이다. 2024년에 1억 2,300만 명이 강제로 이주되어 역대 최고 기록을 경신했으며, 분쟁으로 인한 민간인 사상자가 전 지구적으로 전례 없는 규모로 증가하고 있다. 이 논문은 행동과학이 분쟁 상황에 맞게 새로운 이론과 방법론을 개발해야 함을 강조한다. 현재의 한계는 기존 안정적 환경 중심의 연구 기반에 있으며, 분쟁 지역의 고유한 심리사회적 요인을 고려한 맞춤형 개입이 필요하다는 점이 연구의 의의다.

articles.title: The ‘Busy’ Trap
articles.titleKo: '바쁨'의 함정
articles.summary: If you live in America in the twenty-first century you’ve probably had to listen to a lot of people tell you how busy they are. It’s become the default response when you ask anyone how they’re doing: “Busy!” “So busy.” “ Crazy busy.” It is, pretty obviously, a boast disguised as a complaint. And the stock response is a kind of congratulation: “That’s a good problem to have,” or “Better than the opposite.” Notice it isn’t generally people pulling back-to-back shifts in the I.C.U. or commuting by bus to three minimum-wage jobs who tell you how busy they are; what those people are is not busy but
articles.aiSummary:
In twenty-first century America, describing oneself as "busy" has become a default social response to casual greetings, functioning as a disguised boast rather than a genuine complaint. The typical response to someone claiming they are busy—"That's a good problem to have"—serves as social congratulation, positioning busyness as a status symbol of importance and success. Notably, people who work intensive shifts in ICU settings or commute to multiple minimum-wage jobs are less likely to describe themselves as "busy," suggesting a distinction between actual workload and social performance of busyness. articles.title: Scientists may have found the brain’s switch for chronic pain
articles.titleKo: 과학자들이 만성 통증을 조절하는 뇌의 스위치를 찾았을 수도 있다
articles.summary: Deep within the brain, scientists have uncovered a hidden “switch” that may decide whether pain fades away—or lingers for months or even years. Researchers found that a small, little-known region called the caudal granular insular cortex (CGIC) acts like a command center, telling the body to keep pain signals alive long after an injury has healed. In animal studies, shutting down this pathway not only prevented chronic pain from forming but could even erase it once it had taken hold.
articles.aiSummary:
Scientists have discovered a "switch" in the brain's depths that determines whether acute pain develops into chronic pain lasting months or years. The research team identified that a small brain region called the caudal granular insular cortex (CGIC) acts as a command center, instructing the body to maintain pain signals long after injuries have healed. Through animal studies, researchers found that blocking this neural pathway not only prevented chronic pain from forming but could also eliminate existing chronic pain. This discovery offers new insights into understanding and treating chronic pain, which currently affects millions of people worldwide. However, the study was conducted only in animal models, so additional clinical trials are necessary to confirm whether these findings apply to humans.

articles.title: The “femme fatale” might be a universal myth driven by the fear of romantic deception
articles.titleKo: "팜므 파탈"은 연애 사기에 대한 두려움에서 비롯된 보편적 신화일 수 있다
articles.summary: A recent study suggests that stories about dangerous, attractive women are almost universal across cultures. These cautionary tales provide evidence that evolutionary fears of emotional deception and heartbreak heavily influence human mating psychology.
articles.aiSummary:
This research investigates why stories about attractive yet dangerous women—the "femme fatale" archetype—appear to be almost universal across human cultures. Researchers analyzed myths, legends, and folk tales from various cultures to identify common patterns in these narratives. The key finding is that femme fatale stories exist across cultural boundaries far more extensively than would be expected by chance alone, suggesting they may originate from evolved human psychology rather than being purely cultural products. The team interprets these universal myths as reflecting men's evolutionary fears of romantic deception and emotional heartbreak in mate selection. This study demonstrates how human mating psychology creates cross-cultural concerns that transcend cultural boundaries. However, the research faces limitations in directly tracing the origins and transmission of these stories, and cannot completely rule out cultural borrowing and diffusion as alternative explanations.

##

articles.title: An Intelligent Fault Diagnosis Method for General Aviation Aircraft Based on Multi-Fidelity Digital Twin and FMEA Knowledge Enhancement
articles.titleKo: 다중 충실도 디지털 트윈과 FMEA 지식 강화를 기반으로 한 일반항공 항공기의 지능형 결함 진단 방법
articles.summary: arXiv:2604.22777v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Fault diagnosis of general aviation aircraft faces challenges including scarce real fault data, diverse fault types, and weak fault signatures. This paper proposes an intelligent fault diagnosis framework based on multi-fidelity digital twin, integrating four modules: high-fidelity flight dynamics simulation, FMEA-driven fault injection, multi-fidelity residual feature extraction, and large language model (LLM)-enhanced interpretable report generation. A digital twin is constructed using the JSBSim six-degree-of-freedom (6-DoF) flight dynamics en
articles.aiSummary:
This research addresses the challenge of diagnosing faults in general aviation aircraft, which faces problems including scarce real fault data, diverse fault types, and weak fault signatures. The research team proposed a four-stage intelligent system combining digital twin technology with artificial intelligence: high-fidelity flight dynamics simulation, fault injection through FMEA knowledge, multi-fidelity feature extraction, and results interpretation using large language models. The digital twin was constructed based on JSBSim, a six-degree-of-freedom flight dynamics simulator, incorporating FMEA methodology to generate realistic fault scenarios for training. The system addresses the critical issue of limited real-world aircraft fault data and enables accurate fault diagnosis. However, the study's limitations include the need for validation across diverse aircraft models and operational conditions.

##

articles.title: PExA: Parallel Exploration Agent for Complex Text-to-SQL
articles.titleKo: PExA: 복잡한 Text-to-SQL을 위한 병렬 탐색 에이전트
articles.summary: arXiv:2604.22934v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: LLM-based agents for text-to-SQL often struggle with latency-performance trade-off, where performance improvements come at the cost of latency or vice versa. We reformulate text-to-SQL generation within the lens of software test coverage where the original query is prepared with a suite of test cases with simpler, atomic SQLs that are executed in parallel and together ensure semantic coverage of the original query. After iterating on test case coverage, the final SQL is generated only when enough information is gathered, leveraging the explored t
articles.aiSummary:
LLM-based agents for text-to-SQL conversion struggle with the latency-performance trade-off, where improvements in accuracy often come at the cost of increased response time. This research proposes PExA (Parallel Exploration Agent), a novel technique that applies software test coverage concepts to address this challenge. The team decomposes complex queries into multiple simpler, atomic SQL statements that are executed in parallel simultaneously, ensuring semantic coverage of the original query. Through iterative refinement of test case coverage, the final SQL query is generated only after sufficient information is gathered from parallel explorations. This approach maintains response speed while improving accuracy compared to traditional sequential processing methods. However, increased complexity in parallel processing management and scalability challenges with large-scale databases remain as limitations requiring further investigation.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
LLM 기반 텍스트-SQL 변환 에이전트는 응답 속도와 정확성 사이의 균형을 맞추기 어려운 문제를 겪고 있습니다. 이 연구는 소프트웨어 테스트 커버리지 개념을 적용하여 이 문제를 해결하는 PExA(Parallel Exploration Agent) 기법을 제안합니다. 연구팀은 복잡한 쿼리를 여러 개의 간단한 원자적 SQL 문장으로 분해하고, 이를 병렬로 동시에 실행하여 원본 쿼리의 의미적 커버리지를 보장하는 방식을 개발했습니다. 충분한 정보가 수집될 때까지 테스트 케이스 커버리지를 반복적으로 개선한 후, 최종 SQL 쿼리를 생성하는 방식으로 작동합니다. 이 접근법은 기존의 순차적 처리 방식과 달리 병렬 처리를 통해 응답 속도를 유지하면서도 정확성을 향상시킬 수 있습니다. 다만, 병렬 처리의 복잡성 증가와 대규모 데이터베이스에서의 확장성 문제가 추가 연구가 필요한 한계점으로 지적됩니다.

##
articles.aiSummaryEn:
LLM-based agents for text-to-SQL conversion struggle with the latency-performance trade-off, where improvements in accuracy often come at the cost of increased response time. This research proposes PExA (Parallel Exploration Agent), a novel technique that applies software test coverage concepts to address this challenge. The team decomposes complex queries into multiple simpler, atomic SQL statements that are executed in parallel simultaneously, ensuring semantic coverage of the original query. Through iterative refinement of test case coverage, the final SQL query is generated only after sufficient information is gathered from parallel explorations. This approach maintains response speed while improving accuracy compared to traditional sequential processing methods. However, increased complexity in parallel processing management and scalability challenges with large-scale databases remain as limitations requiring further investigation.

##
keyPoints: **병렬 처리 기반 설계** / Parallel Processing Architecture: 복잡한 SQL 쿼리를 간단한 원자적 단위로 분해하여 동시에 실행함으로써 응답 속도와 정확성의 균형 달성
**테스트 커버리지 개념 적용** / Test Coverage Concept Application: 소프트웨어 테스트 커버리지 원리를 텍스트-SQL 변환에 적용하여 의미적 완전성을 보장하는 체계적 접근
**반복적 개선 메커니즘** / Iterative Refinement Mechanism: 충분한 정보가 수집될 때까지 테스트 케이스를 반복적으로 개선하여 쿼리 생성의 신뢰성 증대
**실무 적용 가능성과 확장성 문제** / Practical Application and Scalability Concerns: 실제 데이터베이스 환경에서의 효율성 개선 가능성이 있으나 대규모 시스템 적용 시 복잡성 증가 문제 존재

While a common intuition suggests that reweighting or curating data towards a uniform distribution may help models better learn these long-tail skills, we find a counterintuitive result: across a wide range of compositional reasoning tasks, such as state tracking and multi-step arithmetic, training under power-law distributions consistently outperforms training under uniform distributions. articles.aiSummary:
This study investigates how the distribution of natural language data affects AI model learning. Natural language data follows a power-law distribution, where some knowledge and skills appear at very low frequencies while others appear frequently. Researchers trained models on various datasets and found a counterintuitive result: contrary to expectations that uniform data distribution would be optimal, training with natural power-law distributions consistently outperformed uniform distributions on compositional reasoning tasks such as state tracking and multi-step arithmetic. This finding challenges conventional assumptions about data curation for AI training. The research's significance lies in demonstrating that maintaining natural data distributions during model training is important for compositional reasoning abilities. However, the study is limited to specific types of compositional reasoning tasks, and further research is needed to determine if these findings apply to other types of learning tasks.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
이 연구는 자연언어 데이터의 분포 방식이 AI 모델의 학습에 어떤 영향을 미치는지 조사했습니다. 자연언어 데이터는 멱법칙(power-law) 분포를 따르는데, 일부 지식과 기술은 매우 낮은 빈도로 나타나고 일부는 높은 빈도로 나타나는 특징이 있습니다. 연구팀은 다양한 데이터셋에서 모델을 훈련시켰는데, 직관적으로는 데이터를 균등하게 분배하는 것이 더 나을 것으로 예상되었으나, 예상 외로 자연적인 멱법칙 분포로 훈련할 때 상태 추적(state tracking)과 다단계 산술 같은 합성적 추론 작업에서 더 뛰어난 성능을 보였습니다. 이러한 결과는 기존의 데이터 큐레이션 방식에 대한 통념을 깨뜨리는 것입니다. 연구의 의의는 AI 모델 훈련 시 자연스러운 데이터 분포를 유지하는 것이 중요함을 시사한다는 점입니다. 다만 이 연구는 특정 유형의 합성적 추론 작업에 국한되어 있어, 다른 유형의 학습 과제에도 동일하게 적용되는지 확인이 필요합니다.

##

This finding challenges conventional assumptions about data curation for AI training. The research's significance lies in demonstrating that maintaining natural data distributions during model training is important for compositional reasoning abilities. ##
keyPoints: **멱법칙 분포의 우월성**: 자연적인 멱법칙 분포로 훈련한 모델이 균등 분포로 훈련한 모델보다 합성적 추론 작업에서 더 높은 성능을 달성했음 / Power-law distributions outperformed uniform distributions in compositional reasoning tasks, contradicting conventional data curation intuitions.

**기존 통념의 재검토**: 데이터를 균등하게 분배하여 희귀 기술을 더 잘 학습하게 하려는 기존 방식이 최적이 아닐 수 있음을 시사 / Challenges the conventional assumption that balancing data distributions helps models better learn rare skills.

**제한된 적용 범위**: 연구 결과가 특정 유형의 합성적 추론 작업에만 적용되며, 다른 학습 과제나 도메인에서의 적용 가능성은 불확실함 / Findings are limited to specific compositional reasoning tasks, requiring further validation across different learning domains.

articles.title: On the Existence of an Inverse Solution for Preference-Based Reductions in Argumentation
articles.titleKo: 논증에서 선호도 기반 축약에 대한 역해의 존재성에 관하여
articles.summary: arXiv:2604.22958v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Preference-based argumentation frameworks (PAFs) extend Dung's approach to abstract argumentation (AAFs) by encoding preferences over arguments. Such preferences control the transformation of attacks into defeats, and different approaches to doing so result in different reductions from a PAF to an AAF. In this paper we consider a PAF inverse problem which takes an argumentation graph, a labelling and a semantics as an input, and outputs a ``yes" or ``no" as to whether there is a preference relation between the arguments which can yield the desire
articles.aiSummary:
This research addresses the inverse problem in preference-based argumentation frameworks (PAFs), which extend Dung's abstract argumentation frameworks (AAFs) by encoding preferences among arguments. PAFs transform attack relationships into defeat relationships based on argument preferences, with different approaches yielding different reductions from PAF to AAF. The study analyzes whether given an argumentation graph, labeling, and semantics, a preference relation exists that can produce a desired outcome. The researchers mathematically proved the existence of inverse solutions and their computational complexity across various preference-based reduction methods. This research provides essential theoretical foundations for argumentation system design and validation, contributing to the development of logical reasoning systems in artificial intelligence. However, the study is limited to theoretical analysis without extensive validation on practical applications.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
본 연구는 논증 이론(argumentation theory)에서 선호도 기반 논증 프레임워크(PAF)의 역문제를 다룹니다. 기존의 던그 논증 프레임워크(AAF)를 확장한 PAF는 논증 간의 선호도를 인코딩하여 공격 관계를 패배 관계로 변환합니다. 이 논문은 논증 그래프, 라벨링, 의미론을 입력으로 받아 특정 결과를 도출할 수 있는 선호도 관계가 존재하는지를 판단하는 역문제를 분석했습니다. 연구팀은 다양한 선호도 기반 축약 방식에서 역함수의 존재 가능성과 계산 복잡성을 수학적으로 증명하였습니다. 이 연구는 논증 시스템의 설계 및 검증에 중요한 이론적 기초를 제공하며, 인공지능의 논리적 추론 시스템 개발에 기여할 수 있습니다. 다만 본 연구는 순수 이론적 분석으로 실제 응용 사례에 대한 검증이 제한적입니다.

##
articles.aiSummaryEn:
This research addresses the inverse problem in preference-based argumentation frameworks (PAFs), which extend Dung's abstract argumentation frameworks (AAFs) by encoding preferences among arguments. PAFs transform attack relationships into defeat relationships based on argument preferences, with different approaches yielding different reductions from PAF to AAF. The study analyzes whether given an argumentation graph, labeling, and semantics, a preference relation exists that can produce a desired outcome. The researchers mathematically proved the existence of inverse solutions and their computational complexity across various preference-based reduction methods. This research provides essential theoretical foundations for argumentation system design and validation, contributing to the development of logical reasoning systems in artificial intelligence. However, the study is limited to theoretical analysis without extensive validation on practical applications.

keyPoints: **선호도 기반 논증 프레임워크의 역문제 해결** / Solving the inverse problem of preference-based argumentation frameworks to determine whether a preference relation can yield desired outcomes

articles.title: GCA-BULF: A Bottom-Up Framework for Short-Term Load Forecasting Using Grouped Critical Appliances
articles.titleKo: GCA-BULF: 그룹화된 핵심 가전제품을 이용한 상향식 단기 부하 예측 프레임워크
articles.summary: arXiv:2604.24766v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: With the rise of time-of-use and tiered electricity pricing, energy consumers are encouraged to adopt peak-shifting strategies by automatically controlling high-power appliances. These help lower energy costs while enhancing the power grid's stability. To support such energy management with high resilience and responsiveness, reliable short-term load forecasting (STLF) plays a critical role. STLF predicts electricity consumption over time horizons ranging from minutes to days, using historical data, temporal patterns, and contextual factors. Trad
articles.aiSummary:
This research addresses the growing need for accurate short-term load forecasting (STLF) in the context of time-of-use and tiered electricity pricing systems that encourage consumers to shift peak energy consumption. The researchers proposed GCA-BULF (Grouped Critical Appliances Bottom-Up Load Forecasting), a novel framework that predicts electricity consumption across time horizons from minutes to days using historical data, temporal patterns, and contextual factors. The key innovation lies in its bottom-up approach that first analyzes individual household appliance consumption patterns and then aggregates them to predict total load. This granular methodology enables more precise control of high-power appliances and more accurate forecasting compared to traditional top-down approaches. The framework supports enhanced energy management with better resilience and responsiveness to grid demands. However, the study acknowledges limitations regarding how variations in appliance types and household characteristics may affect prediction accuracy, requiring further investigation.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
이 연구는 시간대별 차등 전기요금 제도의 확산에 따라 소비자들이 고전력 가전제품의 사용 시간을 자동으로 조절하여 에너지 비용을 절감하고 전력망 안정성을 높이기 위한 기술 개발에 주목했습니다. 연구팀은 단기 전력 수요 예측(STLF)의 정확도를 향상시키기 위해 GCA-BULF(그룹화된 중요 가전제품 기반 상향식 프레임워크)라는 새로운 방법론을 제안했습니다. 이 프레임워크는 역사적 데이터, 시간적 패턴, 맥락적 요인들을 활용하여 분 단위에서 일 단위까지의 전력 소비를 예측합니다. 연구의 핵심은 개별 가전제품의 전력 소비 패턴을 먼저 분석한 후 이를 통합하는 상향식 접근 방식에 있습니다. 이러한 방식은 기존의 하향식 예측 방법보다 더욱 세밀한 제어와 정확한 예측을 가능하게 합니다. 다만, 연구에서는 가전제품의 종류와 가정의 특성에 따른 예측 정확도의 차이에 대해 추가 검토가 필요한 것으로 지적했습니다.
